title: gagea villosa f. uniflora
intro: Gagea villosa f. uniflora, also known as the single-flowered woodlily or one-flowered wood-lily, is a rare and lesser-known species of flowering plant that belongs to the lily family (Liliaceae). The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, and its medicinal properties include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may help to soothe skin irritations, wounds, and infections. Gagea villosa f. uniflora is believed to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and exhibiting antioxidant properties. The plant is typically used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and salves, and its extracts are also used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments.
